title: Pholidia gibsonii
taxonomy: Pholidia gibsonii is a genus of moths in the family Gracillariidae. The family Gracillariidae consists of small, usually unobtrusive moths with long, slender antennae and a relatively long proboscis. Gracillariidae is a family of leaf-mining moths. These moths are often associated with various plant species, including grasses and broad-leaved trees. The classification of Pholidia gibsonii places it within the order Lepidoptera.

intro: Pholidia gibsonii, also known as Yellow Pholidia or Gibson's Yellow Pholidia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. The plant is reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pholidia gibsonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
